locations.name: 路易·皮埃尔·维埃约
locations.summary: 路易·皮埃尔·维埃约（法語：Louis Pierre Vieillot，1748年5月10日—1830年8月24日）是法国的鳥類學家。 维埃约是第一批用卡尔·林奈式二名法科学描述鸟类的研究者之一。至少有26个他命名的鸟类至今仍在学术界使用…

locations.name: 布宜诺斯艾利斯省
locations.summary: 布宜诺斯艾利斯省（西班牙語：Buenos Aires）為南美國家阿根廷二十三省之一，是阿根廷二十四个自治省份（estados autogobernados）之一或者一级司法管辖权（jurisdicciones de primer orde…

locations.name: 常綠森林
locations.summary: 常綠森林是由常綠樹木組成的森林。它們出現在廣泛的氣候帶中，包括寒冷氣候中的針葉樹和冬青樹等樹木，溫帶地區的桉樹、活橡樹、金合歡和班克樹，以及熱帶地區的雨林樹木。針葉溫帶常綠森林最常以科中的物種為主。樹木包括：松科和柏科。闊葉溫帶常綠森林包…

locations.name: Gallery forest
locations.summary: A gallery forest is one formed as a corridor along rivers or wetlands, projecting into landscapes that are otherwise on…

locations.name: 次生林
locations.summary: 次生林，又名天然次生林、再生林、再生森林、二次林。是指原始穩定的森林植被遭到人類活動干擾（刀耕火種、林木砍伐）或同等破壞性的自然現象之後，主要透過自然過程再生形成的森林或林地區域。其特徵有別於未曾遭受近期干擾的原始森林、複雜的早期遷移階段…

locations.name: Várzea forest
locations.summary: A várzea forest is a seasonal floodplain forest inundated by whitewater rivers that occurs in the Amazon biome.

relations.subject: White-winged becard
relations.evidence: The white-winged becard (Pachyramphus polychopterus) is a species of bird in the family Tityridae, the tityras, becards, and allies. It is found in every Central American country except El Salvador, on Trinidad and Tobago, and in every mainland South American country except Chile.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

relations.subject: White-winged becard
relations.evidence: III | title =Birds of Peru | publisher =Princeton University Press | edition =revised and updated |series=Princeton Field Guides | date =2010 | location =Princeton, NJ | pages =480 |isbn = 978-0691130231 }} northwestern Bolivia east across southern Brazil and south through Paraguay, Uruguay, and northern Argentina to Buenos Aires Province The white-winged becard inhabits a wide variety of landscapes in the tropical and lower subtropical zones. Most of them are somewhat open, including the edges and clearings of evergreen forest, gallery and riparian forest, secondary forest, shaded plantations, and river islands
